title: Credentials, Signing, Retries, Timeouts, and Client Resilience · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs credentials, signing, retries, timeouts, and client resilience from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded S3 request and retry state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use short-lived credential providers with explicit workload identity, refresh ownership, least privilege, and redaction.
Explain SigV4 canonical request, region, service, signed headers, payload mode, clock, and credential scope without hand-building production signatures.
Compose request, attempt, transfer, and shutdown deadlines and propagate cancellation through body and goroutine ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Credential Provider Chain · changed case · Review Credential Provider Chain
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 9255 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify S3 outcomes and bound retries while preserving write ambiguity; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use short-lived credential providers with explicit workload identity, refresh ownership, least privilege, and redaction.

Which action demonstrates Credential Provider Chain without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Credential Provider Chain · changed case · Review Credential Provider Chain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use short-lived credential providers with explicit workload identity, refresh ownership, least privilege, and redaction. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Credential Provider Chain · changed case · Review Credential Provider Chain, keep Credential Provider Chain connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make stable identity, conditional requests, upload records, checksums, and post-failure reconciliation explicit for repeatable operations.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Idempotency Reconciliation, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Idempotency Reconciliation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Idempotency Reconciliation, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 1062 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify S3 outcomes and bound retries while preserving write ambiguity; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Make stable identity, conditional requests, upload records, checksums, and post-failure reconciliation explicit for repeatable operations.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Idempotency Reconciliation, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Repeating PutObject after an unknown response cannot create an unintended overwrite.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using make and stable plus specific case evidence.
